name: svg
title: An SVG document fragment consists of any number of SVG elements contained within an 'svg' element.

name: g
title: The g element is a container used to group other SVG elements.

name: defs
title: The 'defs' element is a container element for referenced elements. For understandability and accessibility reasons, it is recommended that, whenever possible, referenced elements be defined inside of a 'defs'.

name: use
title: The 'use' element references another element, a copy of which is rendered in place of the 'use' in the document. The referenced element may be a container element, in which case a copy of the complete SVG document subtree rooted at that element is used.

name: symbol
title: The 'symbol' element is used to define graphical templates which can be instantiated by a 'use' element but which are not rendered directly.

name: rect
title: The 'rect' element defines a rectangle which is axis-aligned with the current user coordinate system. Rounded rectangles can be achieved by setting non-zero values for the 'rx' and 'ry' geometric properties.

name: circle
title: The 'circle' element defines a circle based on a center point and a radius.

name: ellipse
title: The 'ellipse' element defines an ellipse which is axis-aligned with the current user coordinate system based on a center point and two radii.

name: line
title: The 'line' element defines a line segment that starts at one point and ends at another.

name: polyline
title: The 'polyline' element defines a set of connected straight line segments. Typically, 'polyline' elements define open shapes.

name: polygon
title: The 'polygon' element defines a closed shape consisting of a set of connected straight line segments.

name: path
title: A path represents the outline of a shape which can be filled or stroked. A path can also be used as a clipping path, to describe animation, or position text.

name: text
title: The 'text' element defines a graphics element consisting of text. The 'tspan' element within a 'text' or another 'tspan' element, allows one to switch the style and/or adjust the position of the rendered text inside the 'tspan' element relative to the parent element.

name: tspan
title: The tspan element defines a subtext within a text element or another tspan element.

name: textPath
title: SVG can place text along a path defined either by a 'path element' element or the path equivalent of a basic shape. This is specified by including text within a 'textPath' element that has either an 'href' attribute with an URL reference pointing to a 'path element' element or basic shape, or by specifying a value for the 'path' attribute that specifies the path data directly.

name: a
title: SVG provides an 'a' element, to indicate links (also known as hyperlinks or Web links). An 'a' element forms a link if it has a 'href' or 'xlink:href' attribute; without these attributes the 'a' element is an inactive placeholder for a link.

name: image
title: The 'image' element indicates that the contents of a complete file are to be rendered into a given rectangle within the current user coordinate system. The 'image' element can refer to raster image files such as PNG or JPEG or to files with MIME type of "image/svg+xml".

name: title
title: The title element provides an accessible, short-text description of any SVG container element or graphics element.

name: desc
title: The desc element provides an accessible, long-text description of any SVG container element or graphics element.

name: metadata
title: Metadata which is included with SVG content should be specified within 'metadata' elements. The contents of the 'metadata' should be elements from other XML namespaces, with these elements from these namespaces expressed in a manner conforming with the Namespaces in XML Recommendation [xml-names].

name: clipPath
title: The clipPath element defines a clipping path that can be applied to other elements via the clip-path property.

name: mask
title: The mask element defines an alpha mask for compositing the current object into the background.

name: linearGradient
title: Linear gradients are defined by a 'linearGradient' element.

name: radialGradient
title: Radial gradients are defined by a 'radialGradient' element.

name: stop
title: The vector of colors to use in a gradient is defined by the 'stop' elements that are child elements to a 'linearGradient', or 'radialGradient' element.

name: pattern
title: The pattern element defines a graphics object which can be redrawn at repeated x- and y-coordinate intervals to cover an area.

name: filter
title: The filter element serves as container for atomic filter operations, defined by filter primitives.

name: feGaussianBlur
title: The feGaussianBlur filter primitive performs a Gaussian blur on the input image.

name: foreignObject
title: SVG is designed to be compatible with other XML languages for describing and rendering other types of content. The 'foreignObject' element allows for inclusion of elements in a non-SVG namespace which is rendered within a region of the SVG graphic using other user agent processes.

name: style
title: The style element allows style sheets to be embedded directly within SVG content.

name: script
title: A 'script' element is equivalent to the 'script' element in HTML and thus is the place for scripts (e.g., ECMAScript). Any functions defined within any 'script' element have a "global" scope across the entire current document.

name: marker
title: The 'marker element' element defines the graphics that are to be used for drawing markers on a shape.

name: switch
title: The 'switch' element evaluates the 'requiredExtensions' and 'systemLanguage' attributes on its direct child elements in order, and then processes and renders the first child for which these attributes evaluate to true. All others will be bypassed and therefore not rendered.

name: animate
title: The 'animate' element is used to animate a single attribute or property over time.

name: animateMotion
title: The 'animateMotion' element causes a referenced element to move along a motion path.

name: animateTransform
title: The 'animateTransform' element animates a transformation attribute on a target element, thereby allowing animations to control translation, scaling, rotation and/or skewing.

name: set
title: The 'set' element provides a simple means of just setting the value of an attribute for a specified duration. It supports all attribute types, including those that cannot reasonably be interpolated, such as string and boolean values.

name: mpath
title: The mpath element provides the ability to reference an external path as the definition of a motion path for animateMotion.

name: feDistantLight
title: The feDistantLight filter primitive defines a distant light source that can be used within a lighting filter primitive.

name: fePointLight
title: The fePointLight filter primitive defines a point light source that can be used within a lighting filter primitive.

name: feSpotLight
title: The feSpotLight filter primitive defines a spot light source that can be used within a lighting filter primitive.

name: feBlend
title: The feBlend filter primitive composites two objects together by the specified blending mode.

name: feColorMatrix
title: The feColorMatrix filter primitive applies a matrix transformation on color values.

name: feComponentTransfer
title: The feComponentTransfer filter primitive allows component-wise remapping of the input image.

name: feComposite
title: The feComposite filter primitive performs combination of images using Porter-Duff compositing operations.

name: feConvolveMatrix
title: The feConvolveMatrix filter primitive applies a matrix convolution filter effect.

name: feDiffuseLighting
title: The feDiffuseLighting filter primitive lights an image using its alpha channel as a bump map.

name: feDisplacementMap
title: The feDisplacementMap filter primitive uses pixel values from the second input to spatially displace the first input.

name: feFlood
title: The feFlood filter primitive fills the subregion with a single color and opacity.

name: feImage
title: The feImage filter primitive fetches image data from an external source and provides the pixel data as output.

name: feMerge
title: The feMerge filter primitive composites input image layers on top of each other.

name: feMergeNode
title: The feMergeNode element represents one input layer within a feMerge composition.

name: feMorphology
title: The feMorphology filter primitive thins or fattens the input image.

name: feOffset
title: The feOffset filter primitive offsets the input image.

name: feSpecularLighting
title: The feSpecularLighting filter primitive lights a source graphic using its alpha channel as a bump map, producing a specular reflection.

name: feTile
title: The feTile filter primitive fills a target rectangle with a repeated, tiled pattern of an input image.

name: feTurbulence
title: The feTurbulence filter primitive creates an image using the Perlin turbulence function.

name: feFuncR
title: The feFuncR element defines the transfer function for the red component.

name: feFuncG
title: The feFuncG element defines the transfer function for the green component.

name: feFuncB
title: The feFuncB element defines the transfer function for the blue component.

name: feFuncA
title: The feFuncA element defines the transfer function for the alpha component.

name: feDropShadow
title: The feDropShadow SVG element.

name: view
title: The 'view' element is defined as follows:
